title: maerua oblongifolia
intro: Maerua oblongifolia, also known as the 'bitter apple', is a plant species native to tropical Africa, characterized by its bitter and astringent properties. The medicinal properties of Maerua oblongifolia are attributed to its ability to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The health benefits of the plant include its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antifungal properties, which can help in reducing pain, inflammation, and promoting wound healing. Maerua oblongifolia can be prepared as an infusion or decoction for medicinal purposes, and it is also used in traditional African medicine to treat various health conditions.
